stem: A application enters the immigration pipeline. The probability of passing document verification is 78%. If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing background check is between 79% and 91% (uncertain due to evaluator variability). If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing interview is 93%. What is the overall probability that the application is approved?

ground_truth.answer: P(approved) ranges from 0.573 to 0.66 as the background check pass rate varies from 79% to 91%. Point estimate: 0.617.

solution_steps: The application must pass all 3 stages sequentially: P(success) = P(stage 1)×P(stage 2)×P(stage 3).
The uncertainty in the background check stage creates a 8.7 percentage point range in the overall success probability.
common_failure_modes: Adding probabilities instead of multiplying for sequential stages
Using a single value for the background check rate without acknowledging uncertainty
Not recognizing that uncertainty at one stage propagates through all subsequent stages
